_comment: sovereign-os compliance dashboard (R489, R458+). Visualizes the operator-§1g/§1h compliance aggregator that consolidates the 4-instrument suite (surface-map / doc-coverage / anti-minimization-audit / ux-design-audit) into a single accountability view. Sourced from sovereign_os_operator_compliance_query_total{verb,instrument,result} (Layer B, SDD-016) emitted by scripts/operator/compliance.py. Operator imports into Grafana via Settings → JSON Model. Closes the future compliance-dashboard waiver and registers compliance in MODULE_COVERAGE at 3 surfaces (core/cli/dashboard).

panels.title: status verb (count)
panels.description: One-screen rollup — operator's primary 'where am I on compliance?' verb.

panels.title: worst verb (count)
panels.description: Top-N worst modules by composite gap score.

panels.title: snapshot verb (count)
panels.description: Triple-gated snapshots persisted to the journal.

panels.title: compliance query rate per verb (5m)
panels.description: Per-verb query rate over time. status / module / worst / history / snapshot — operator's compliance-tooling usage pattern.

panels.title: compliance instrument attention (5m)
panels.description: Per-instrument query rate. surface-map / doc-coverage / anti-minimization-audit / ux-design-audit / all — operator-revealed-preference signal for which axis is most-watched.

panels.title: compliance result distribution
panels.description: ok / preview / applied / dry-run / write-failed — operator-§1g result-class breakdown across all verbs.

panels.title: compliance current state (verb × instrument × result)
panels.description: Per-(verb, instrument, result) histogram — operator-discoverable: 'which compliance instrument am I exercising most, and is it passing?'

options.content:
**Operator §1g/§1h verbatim** (R458 — anti-minimization standing rule):

> "We do not minimize anything."

This aggregator IS the §1g operator-discoverable accountability dashboard. Compliance is the SINGLE screen the operator looks at to answer: 'where is the sovereign-os ↔ operator-§1g delivery contract being silently shortened?'

**4-instrument suite (read-only delegation):**

1. `surface-map` (R453, E11.M3) — runtime-surface coverage per module
   - 8-surface ladder: core / cli / tui / api / mcp / dashboard / webapp / service
   - Threshold = 3 surfaces (modules below threshold trigger surface-gap audit)
2. `doc-coverage` (R454, E11.M1) — doc-surface coverage per module
   - 7-kind ladder: readme / sdd / helptext / metric-inventory / mandate-row / man-page
3. `anti-minimization-audit` (R456, E11.M11) — cross-axis + source-code minimization patterns
   - 8 patterns: todo-no-anchor / empty-stub / skipped-no-followup / surface-gap / doc-gap / mandate-todo / minimize-phrase / partial-status
   - All 8 patterns currently CLEAN (R480+R484 closed the last shortfalls)
4. `ux-design-audit` (R457, E11.M10) — 6-dimension UX quality per module
   - 6 dimensions: action-budget / discoverable / recoverable / next-step / operator-named / readable-30s

**Operator-discoverable composite gap score:** `worst` verb returns top-N modules sorted by composite (lower = worse) across all 4 instruments. The operator's daily question: 'which modules are silently shortening the contract today?'

**Snapshot journal:**

`/var/lib/sovereign-os/compliance/snapshots.jsonl` — operator-triple-gated append-only journal of compliance state over time. Drives the `history` verb + operator-discoverable trend analysis.

**CLI:**

```
sovereign-osctl compliance status
sovereign-osctl compliance module <name>
sovereign-osctl compliance worst [--limit N]
sovereign-osctl compliance history [--limit N]
sovereign-osctl compliance snapshot --apply --confirm-snapshot
```

**Metric:** `sovereign_os_operator_compliance_query_total{verb,instrument,result}` (SDD-016 Layer B)

**Source:** R458 shipped the verbs + 4-instrument aggregator. R489 (R458+) ships THIS dashboard + registers compliance as a first-class module in MODULE_COVERAGE at 3 surfaces (core/cli/dashboard).

**Standing operator rule (sacrosanct):**

> "We do not minimize anything."

title: sovereign-os compliance
